place_address: police station, Byculla West, Byculla, Mumbai, Maharashtra 400011, India
text:
Byculla Fruit Market, situated in the Sant Gadge Maharaj Mandai, is one of Mumbai's oldest and most vibrant wholesale markets, renowned for its extensive selection of fresh fruits and vegetables.  Operating from early morning hours, the market becomes a bustling hub where traders and customers engage in lively negotiations, reflecting the energetic atmosphere of Mumbai's traditional marketplaces.

Highlights

Diverse Produce: The market offers a wide variety of fruits and vegetables, catering to both individual shoppers and bulk buyers.

Historical Significance: Established in 1939 and expanded in 1960, the market holds historical value and has been a central point for fresh produce in Mumbai for decades.

Challenges

Food Waste: Some visitors have expressed concerns about the significant amount of food waste observed in the market, suggesting a need for better waste management practices.

Infrastructure Developments: Ongoing construction of a new road over-bridge has led to the relocation of several fruit vendors within the market premises, causing temporary disruptions.

Visiting Tips

Timing: For the best selection, consider visiting early in the morning when the market is most active.

Bargaining: Engaging in price negotiations is common and can lead to better deals.

Navigation: Due to its bustling nature, it's advisable to be cautious and aware of your surroundings while navigating through the market.

text:
I wanted to express my dissatisfaction with my recent experience while attempting to place an order for Dhokla. The price for 1 kg was quoted at ₹400, which I was willing to accept, but I only wanted to order 1/2 kg (₹200). Despite the offer of "free delivery," the staff at the counter informed me that the minimum delivery charge is ₹300. She then suggested I purchase additional items (chocolate or biscuits worth ₹100) to meet the minimum.

I was then called a few minutes later and told that, since a delivery was already scheduled in my area, I would need to add an additional ₹50 to my order.

I understand that your store may be aiming to increase sales, but this approach feels misleading and not customer-friendly. Instead of encouraging additional purchases through these tactics, it only led to mom trust the store

I hope you reconsider how you handle such policies in the future, as this experience was very disappointing.

text:
Please make sure to carefully check all manufacturing and expiry dates before purchasing anything from this store. Many of the “Buy One Get One” and heavily discounted items are close to expiry. Some products have expiry dates within just 15 days. For example, their Real juices and certain biscuits that were stacked as part of the Ramadan offers are nearing expiry.

Please be extra cautious during Ramzan. Consuming products that are close to expiry can pose health risks, especially when they are being promoted as festive offers. Imagine the risk to someone who doesn’t read labels carefully or unknowingly consumes such items.

I would also like to specifically mention a staff member named Sunil, who was extremely rude and displayed a lot of ego. He misbehaved with my mother. When I raised a complaint with the owner and requested action, I received a call from their team, and Sunil was asked to apologise. However, his apology lacked sincerity and professionalism.

If you happen to visit the store, please be mindful of this salesperson. His behaviour was inappropriate and uncomfortable.

PS: Please be careful and always read labels before purchasing anything from this store. My suggestion would be to use trusted online delivery apps or your local kirana store, where you may receive better discounts and avoid the risk of being sold near-expiry products.

place_address: Shop no. 101, Anand Amrut, Tejpal Rd, near Parle Biscuit Factory, Vishnu Prasad Society, Navpada, Vile Parle East, Vile Parle, Mumbai, Maharashtra 400057, India
text:
One of the best-managed and most responsible electronic stores in Mumbai. I would rate it among the finest supermarkets across Mumbai and the suburbs. They have everything — from basic home essentials to mobile phones, white goods, and even Smart Home Automation. Their focus on quality is clear from the premium brands they stock.

The store is run by young and dynamic entrepreneurs, Manishji and Rajeshbhai, guided by their highly respected father Pankajbhai. Their values, tradition, and humble attitude truly set them apart. The staff is well-organised, professional, and always ready to guide you with the right product suggestions.

I’ve been a customer for over 28 years and have seen their dedication and growth firsthand. Despite their success, the family remains humble, polite, and extremely customer-friendly.

Highly recommended. Wishing them continued success.

text:
I am writing to express my concern about an issue I faced during my recent visit to your store (Kandivali East Thakur village).

On September 24th 2025 , I made a purchase of ₹7,556, which included groceries, clothing, and other items. On my recent visit to your store on 4th October, I returned with my wife to buy matching leggings for a kurta she had purchased earlier. While checking in, your staff tied my carry bag, as per your store policy.

However, when I exited the store, the security alarm went off, and it was discovered that a security tag had not been removed from one of the items I purchased on 24th September, 2025. This was clearly an oversight from your staff.

It is the responsibility of your staff to ensure that all security tags are properly removed at the time of billing. Their failure to do so caused this unnecessary inconvenience.

Additionally, the behavior of your security staff was disappointing. One female security staff member was particularly rude and unprofessional. Rather than handling the situation calmly, she blamed me for the issue, which was both frustrating and embarrassing. To make things worse, another staff member chose to argue with me instead of checking the security camera to verify the situation. This only delayed the process further and made it more uncomfortable.

The entire incident was time-consuming, unpleasant, and left me feeling disrespected as a customer.

Since the item was purchased by me and the bag was sealed by your staff, I believe I should not have been subjected to this kind of treatment. I request that you take appropriate action to ensure such incidents do not happen again, and that your team is trained to handle these situations more professionally and respectfully.

I look forward to your response and a resolution to this matter.

Thank you!

Terrible Service: 45+ Minute Wait for a Simple Exchange!

My recent experience at Reliance Smart Bazaar was appalling, specifically regarding an exchange request. I was made to stand for over 45 minutes just to process a simple product exchange.This inexcusable delay was a direct result of staff negligence at the second enterance(near elevator) where I was not prompted for any baggage checks upon enterance with the product I wanted to exchange at the first place and at the cash counter the blame was pasted upon me for shoplifting (though had the proper receipts of the product I desired to exchange.)The employees handling the exchange process seemed disorganized, poorly trained, and completely unable to efficiently manage the task at hand. There was no sense of urgency or accountability, and multiple staff members seemed involved yet accomplished nothing for an extremely long period.
Waiting three-quarters of an hour for what should be a 5-minute transaction is unacceptable and shows a complete disregard for the customer's time. Reliance Smart Bazaar needs to urgently address the training and competency of their customer service and exchange desk staff. I should not have to waste nearly an hour of my day because your employees cannot perform basic tasks efficiently.
Extremely disappointed and unlikely to shop here again if this is the level of service one can expect. Avoid this store if you value your time.

text:
This place which opened a few months back is the largest store for groceries and other household products in Thakur Village Kandivali. It is in direct competition with the DMart store which has had a competition free run for the past 15 years or so in the absence of any other Super Market in Thakur Village.
The place is quite large and there are separate sections for different types of produce required for running a house.
In addition to the usual groceries therWe are sections for garments, kitchenware, vegetables and fruits, dairy products, fresh snacks & beverages.
One needs a few visits to get a handle on  the location in which different items are kept. The choice of brands for commonly used household groceries is definitely more than what is available in any other store, so one is spoilt for choices.
Also unique is the fact that staple groceries like foodgrains, pulses, sugar, vegetables, fruits etc are available in loose form which makes it convenient for people to buy the exact quantity by weight which the family requires.
Parking space in the basement and toilets add to a convenient shopping experience.
Will update with pictures shortly

place_address: Shop 1 TO 5, Aayushi Co-Op. Hsg. Soc., Ltd, B Wing, S.B. Road, Bamanpuri, S B Singh Colony, J B Nagar, Andheri East, Mumbai, Maharashtra 400059, India
text:
I am extremely disappointed with the service at Noble Chemist. The service is very poor and unprofessional.
I was standing in line to purchase urgent medicines for a diabetes patient, but the staff completely ignored my request. Instead of prioritizing medicines, they were focused on selling other products like beverages, chips, and ice creams to other customers.
Even after I repeatedly informed the staff that the medicine was very urgent, they did not take it seriously. When I requested them to please provide the medicine quickly, the staff member showed attitude and responded rudely, saying, “I am doing my work, please don’t tell me.”
This is unacceptable behavior, especially in a medical store where medicines should be the top priority. Patients who need urgent medication should not be ignored while other non-essential products are being sold.
Very disappointing experience. I hope the management takes this seriously and improves their service.
